channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: China deploys record 125 warplanes in large scale military drill in warning to Taiwan
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China employed a record 125 aircraft, as well as its Liaoning aircraft carrier and ships, in large-scale military exercises surrounding Taiwan and its outlying islands Monday, simulating the sealing off of key ports in a move that underscores the tense situation in the Taiwan S
item.dc:creator: JOHNSON LAI and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strike on hospital tent camp kills 4 and ignites a fire that burns dozens
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli airstrike on a hospital courtyard in the Gaza Strip early Monday killed at least four people and triggered a fire that swept through a tent camp for people displaced by the war, leaving more than two dozen with severe burns, according to Palestinian medics
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN peacekeepers in southern Lebanon are in the crosshairs of Israel’s war on Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — As the war between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah group escalates, a United Nations peacekeeping force in Lebanon is increasingly in the crosshairs, with Israeli troops firing at the peacekeepers' headquarters and positions several times in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Netanyahu mulls plan to empty northern Gaza of civilians and cut off aid to those left inside
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is examining a plan to seal off humanitarian aid to northern Gaza in an attempt to starve out Hamas militants, a plan that, if implemented, could trap without food or water hundreds of thousands of Palestinians unwilling or unable to leave t
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: North Korea is preparing to destroy northern parts of inter-Korean roads, Seoul says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea said Monday it has detected signs that North Korea is preparing to destroy the northern parts of inter-Korean roads no longer in use, as the rivals are embroiled in soaring tensions over North Korea’s claim that South Korea flew drones over its territory.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: France’s far-right leader Marine Le Pen faces court on charges of embezzling EU funds
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French far-right leader Marine Le Pen is to answer questions from judges at a Paris court Monday as she and her National Rally party stand trial over the suspected embezzlement of European Parliament funds. She has denied any wrongdoing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden-Harris admin accused of ‘too little, too late’ to save the people of war-torn, famine-stricken Sudan
item.description: Critics say the Biden administration is in danger of waiting too long to stop the killing and help the people of Sudan get humanitarian aid, as it makes a last-minute effort to make a difference.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel says 4 soldiers killed by Hezbollah drone attack while Israeli strike in Gaza leaves 20 dead
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hezbollah drone attack on an army base in central Israel killed four soldiers and severely wounded seven others Sunday, the military said, in the deadliest strike by the militant group since Israel launched its ground invasion of Lebanon nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ailing and silenced in prison, Belarus activist symbolizes the nation's repression
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — The last time any of Maria Kolesnikova's family had contact with the imprisoned Belarusian opposition activist was more than 18 months ago. Fellow inmates at the penal colony reported hearing her plead for medical help from inside her tiny and smelly cell.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea sends warning to South Korea, saying troops ready to strike if more drones appear
item.description: North Korea said its army units are prepared to launch strikes against South Korea if another drone carrying propaganda flyers flies into the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Macao's former top judge is chosen as the Chinese casino hub's first leader born in mainland China
item.description: MACAO (AP) — Macao’s former top judge, Sam Hou Fai, was chosen as the Chinese casino hub’ s next leader in a largely ceremonial election on Sunday, setting him up to become the city’s first chief executive born in mainland China.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine’s human rights envoy urges response to alleged killings of Ukrainian POWs in Kursk
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine’s human rights ombudsman urged international organizations Sunday to respond to a claim that several Ukrainian prisoners of war were executed in Russia’s Kursk region, where Kyiv had launched an incursion in August.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gender of female captain not to blame for sinking of $61M navy ship: New Zealand defense minister
item.description: New Zealand's Defense Minister has rebukes claims that the appointment of a female to captain a $61 million navy ship ultimately led to its sinking.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: British YouTuber and rapper Yung Filly is charged with raping and choking a woman in Australia
item.description: PERTH, Australia (AP) — A British YouTuber and rapper known as Yung Filly has been charged with raping and choking a woman in a hotel room following an Australian music performance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rome mayor responds after Macron says he'll fight to keep 'Emily in Paris' in France: 'Netflix does not take orders from heads of state'
item.description: "Emily in Paris" is sparking a très dramatique back-and-forth between the French president and the mayor of Rome, who are battling about the Netflix series potentially bidding adieu to the City of Light.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: Yemen’s Houthi rebels attack a Liberian-flagged ship in the Red Sea
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels attacked a Liberian-flagged chemical tanker on Thursday in the Red Sea, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Brown votes against student proposal to divest from companies allegedly connected to war in Gaza
item.description: Brown University announced Thursday it will not divest from companies that pro-Palestinian students say contribute to Israel's war efforts in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Archaeologists unearth unexpected find inside a tomb likely belonging to a Roman gladiator
item.description: In Turkey, archaeologists unearthed a tomb seemingly belonging to a Roman gladiator of the third century B.C., but were surprised to find the remains of 12 individuals inside.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump-Putin ties face fresh scrutiny with Woodward book
item.description: Less than a month before the U.S. presidential election, former President Trump is facing fresh scrutiny on his relationship with Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Canadian woman charged with killing 3 people in 3 days, labeled a serial killer
item.description: A Canadian woman allegedly killed three people in separate incidents over three days in and around Toronto, authorities said last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli defense minister warns an attack on Iran would be 'lethal' and 'surprising'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel's defense minister warned on Wednesday that his country's retaliation for a recent Iranian missile attack will be “lethal” and “surprising,” while the Israeli military pushed ahead with a large-scale operation in northern Gaza and a ground offensive in Lebanon against Hezboll
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: The Kremlin confirmed that former President Trump while in office sent Russian President Vladimir Putin COVID-19 testing devices during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: The history of the Hoxne Hoard, the largest collection of Roman treasure found in Britain
item.description: The Hoxne Hoard is one of the most significant archaeological finds out of Britain. The hoard is mostly made of gold and silver coins, although other unique objects add to its value.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel hit with multiple terrorist attacks as Hezbollah rocket kills 2 Israelis while walking dogs
item.description: Israel was hit with another round of terrorist attacks as Hezbollah fired rockets into a northern border town, killing two, and knife-wielding moped driver attacked in Hadera, putting eight in the hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in Madeleine McCann disappearance case acquitted of unrelated sexual offense charges
item.description: A German court has acquitted Christian Brueckner, who is also under investigation in the disappearance of British toddler Madeleine McCann, in a trial over unrelated sexual offenses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian court sentences a 72-year-old American to nearly 7 years in prison for fighting in Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Monday sentenced a 72-year-old American in a closed trial to nearly seven years in prison for allegedly fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea's Kim again threatens to use nuclear weapons against South Korea and US
item.description: The latest warning came as experts say North Korea could ramp up hostilities ahead of the U.S. presidential election.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation
